title: tax — retention windows
description: Tax calculations are financial records — 7 years (10 for Enterprise). Tax rates + jurisdictions retain indefinitely for historical audit (calculation-freeze depends on the rate being resolvable years later). Exemptions retain 7 years post-expiry. TenantErased migrates calculations to compliance archive rather than hard-deleting.
compliance_regimes_referenced: IRS Publication 583 — 7-year business tax record retention (US)
EU VAT Directive Art. 244 — records retention for VAT (typically 10 years for some regimes)
UK HMRC — 6-year record retention (extended to 10 for certain sectors)
GDPR Art. 17(3)(b) — carve-out: legal obligation supersedes erasure right
PCI-DSS 10.7 — 1 year hot + 6 years archive for audit trail
SOC 2 CC7.4 — restore + retention procedures documented

hot.description: Active rates + first 10 years of historical rates hot for calculation-freeze lookups. Old rates are hit rarely but must be resolvable for audit.

cold.description: Historical rates NEVER hard-deleted. They anchor calculation-freeze snapshots — dropping them would corrupt the audit trail.

purge.notes: Tenant erasure removes tenant-scoped rates. Platform-reference rates (there are none — every rate is tenant-scoped) would never be purged.
Tenant-scoped custom jurisdictions cascade on tenant erasure.

purge.trigger: manual (super_admin only, via database migration + audit)
purge.notes: Platform-reference jurisdictions are effectively immortal — they underpin every tenant's calculations. Removing one requires a coordinated migration + audit event.

hot.description: Active exemptions + first 2 years post-expiry hot for calculation-freeze lookups

cold.description: Regulator audit retention: 7 years past the exemption's expiry date.

purge.notes: Exemptions valid_until IS NULL retain indefinitely (perpetual exemptions — rare, typically diplomatic). Certificate documents in S3 are purged in the same sweep via CertificateStorage::purge().

hot.description: Active + first year for reporting, support queries, dispute resolution

cold.description: Financial-record retention. Enterprise tenants with tax_extended_retention entitlement: 3650 (10 years).

purge.trigger: > 7 years past calculated_at (10 years for Enterprise)
purge.action: MIGRATE to tax_calculations_archive (linked to invoice_lines_archive) — NEVER hard-delete inside the module
purge.notes: This is the module's most-important retention rule. Tax calculations NEVER hard-delete inside the module — they migrate to compliance archive. The archive retention runs 10 years from calculation date (matches the most-restrictive regulator regime). Regulator audits can reach into the archive via the compliance module's data-subject-rights surface.

purge.action: cascaded (blobs are sub-fields, not separate tables)
purge.notes: The snapshots outlive individual rate changes / exemption revocations on the source rows — that's the whole point of the freeze pattern. The calculation carries its historical rate + exemption for its lifetime.
90-day grace allows rollback after credential rotation errors. After grace, credentials are purged but the historical config data survives in the audit trail (7 years).
Certificate documents in S3 use the same retention as their parent exemption row. Deletion is via CertificateStorage which writes an audit tombstone (regulators may need proof of deletion).

special_cases.action: MigrateCalculationsToArchiveOnTenantErased hook (priority 20) migrates tax_calculations to tax_calculations_archive BEFORE PurgeTaxDataForErasedTenant (priority 50) cascades other tax tables
special_cases.notes: The FK CASCADE on tax_calculations.tenant_id is a defensive fallback — the archive migration should always complete first. Without the archive migration, tax_calculations would be hard-deleted on tenant erasure, corrupting regulator audit trail.

special_cases.action: RedactUserFromTaxRecords nulls customer_id on tax_exemptions WHERE customer_type='User' AND customer_id=erased_user.id. tax_calculations preserve the calculation record with any subject-identifying fields in metadata nulled.
special_cases.notes: Art. 17(3)(b) carve-out — tax record retention is a legal obligation that supersedes erasure right. The core tax_amount + tax_rate_snapshot + calculated_at survive.

special_cases.action: ReencryptCertificateUrlsOnKeyRotation background sweep re-encrypts every tax_exemptions.certificate_document_url with the new key. Runs chunked to avoid blocking.
special_cases.notes: SOC 2 CC6.7 encryption key rotation. Every 90 days by default.

cross_reference: The audit rows written by Auditable outlive the source rows per audit::retention.json — audit retention is 7 years regardless of source-row retention.
TenantErased cascade migrates calculations to compliance archive via a priority-20 hook that runs BEFORE the FK CASCADE.

The compliance/data-subject-rights module orchestrates UserErased across all modules — the tax module registers `RedactUserFromTaxRecords` as a compliance callback with the Art. 17(3)(b) carve-out semantics.
